title: [InstCombine] Fixing wrong select folding in vectors with undef elements
summary: The developers generalized the optimization by removing an overly restrictive check that blocked constant expressions, allowing the transformation to handle a broader range of constant vector conditions. Previously, the code skipped all `ConstantExpr` elements, which prevented clearing demanded bits even when the condition was clearly true or false. The fix leverages `isOneValue()` to positively identify true conditions, enabling the same logic to work for any constant that evaluates to one, including constant expressions. This transforms a narrow fix for a specific `undef` case into a robust pattern that simplifies demanded element analysis for any constant vector select where condition elements are provably true or false. The key insight: avoid blanket exclusions; instead, use precise predicates (`isNullValue`, `isOneValue`) to safely generalize.
